title: Tool Registry, Allowlist, Validation, Dispatch, Results, and Unknown Calls · Retrieval Circuit
summary: A delayed engineering review reconstructs tool registry, allowlist, validation, dispatch, results, and unknown calls from stakeholder, revision, runtime, model, interaction, call, result, state, approval, evaluation, trace, failure, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for allowlisted tool registry and result envelope after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Register immutable tool name, schema version, handler, effect class, policy, timeout, owner, and result contract in one auditable catalog.
Preserve provider call ID, local attempt ID, interaction ID, tool version, arguments digest, result digest, and timestamps.
Validate and normalize model arguments before policy and execution while retaining rejected input evidence safely.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-ai-agent-python cumulative safe evaluated AI agent portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python interaction, tool, policy, state, memory,
evaluation, telemetry, and recovery models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only.
Provider APIs, Gemini models, native google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic, HTTP, credentials,
host files, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, cost, privacy review,
and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentPolicy:
    stakeholder_task_id: str
    allowed_tool_names: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_steps: int = 12
    maximum_tool_calls: int = 8
    approval_required_for_writes: bool = True


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentEvidence:
    revision: str
    model_config_digest: str
    policy_digest: str
    evaluation_digest: str
    recovery_drill_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Safe evaluated Python AI agent portfolio ready"

steps.instruction:
review case — A review agent engineering team handles case 4007 during a controlled support-operations rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to dispatch only allowlisted validated tools while preserving call identity, result envelopes, unknown-tool rejection, and exactly-once evidence; telemetry and memory must remain tenant-isolated, minimized, redacted, and attributable; then change model capability, API version, schema, or provider state and revalidate the adapter. Browser code uses pure bounded state machines and original fixed fixtures only. Real provider APIs, Gemini models, google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic native validation, HTTP, files, credentials, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, privacy review, and production effects require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Reject unknown, disabled, stale, unauthorized, and version-mismatched tools without dynamic resolution or side effects.

Inspect the Unknown Tool Rejection trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

stimulus.title: Unknown Tool Rejection case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A review agent engineering team handles case 4007 during a controlled support-operations rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to dispatch only allowlisted validated tools while preserving call identity, result envelopes, unknown-tool rejection, and exactly-once evidence; telemetry and memory must remain tenant-isolated, minimized, redacted, and attributable; then change model capability, API version, schema, or provider state and revalidate the adapter. lines.label: risk
lines.text: A model-selected tool should be invoked if any similarly named function exists.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Reject unknown, disabled, stale, unauthorized, and version-mismatched tools without dynamic resolution or side effects. Artifact under review: allowlisted tool registry and result envelope.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Unknown Tool Rejection, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Unknown Tool Rejection record says allowlisted tool registry and result envelope looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary for Unknown Tool Rejection.

For separate evidence from confidence, run one convenient fluent model response, omit stakeholder risk, revision, model and API identity, interaction and call correlation, tool schema and authority, state and resource budgets, approval, changed fixtures, injection, privacy, accessibility, evaluation, recovery, and controlled provider transfer, then accept plausible text as agent proof. This does not satisfy Unknown Tool Rejection for allowlisted tool registry and result envelope.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Reject unknown, disabled, stale, unauthorized, and version-mismatched tools without dynamic resolution or side effects.
